<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249569553 space=MMA version=3 -->
## PAGE 00658: Model simulation

- page_id: `249569553`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249569553/Model+simulation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-07T15:31:40.902+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide
- labels: ['model-simulation', 'simulating-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to run elements simulation in a MagicDraw project. Elements that can be simulated are those that are supported by the simulation engines in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Any number of simulation engines can be implemented as separate plugins and registered to Simulation Framework as the engines for some particular types of models.

The following table shows a list of simulation engines and their supported elements that come with Cameo Simulation Toolkit

| Simulation engine | Supported elements |
| --- | --- |
| Activity engine | Activities.Activity diagrams.Classes whose classifier behavior is an Activity.InstanceSpecifications of a class whose classifier behavior is an Activity. |
| State Machine engine | State Machines.State Machine diagrams.Classes whose classifier behavior is a State Machine.InstanceSpecifications of a Class whose classifier behavior is a State Machine. |
| Interaction engine | Interactions.Sequence diagrams.Classes whose classifier behavior is an Interaction.InstanceSpecification of a Class whose classifier behavior is an Interaction. |
| Parametric engine | Blocks that contain properties that are bound to other properties (or constraint parameters) with connected binding connectors.SysML Parametric diagrams.InstanceSpecifications of a Block containing properties that are bound to other properties (or constraint parameters) with connected binding connectors. |

To run a simulation, do one of the following

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements simulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running a Simulation Configuration' space=''] including setting a target element to be simulated by the Simulation Configuration and run the model from the Simulation Configuration.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity simulation engine' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine simulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction simulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Parametric evaluator' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249569703 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00659: Model-based clock

- page_id: `249569703`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249569703/Model-based+clock
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:06.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Simulation time and simulation clock
- labels: ['model-based-clock']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1350049295

1350049298

1350049296

Occasionally, you may want to have full control over the amount of simulation time to simulate your model. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to use a value of any selected property to determine the simulation time. With this option, you can control the value of the simulation time with your model. The model that enables you to use the runtime value as the simulation time is called "model-based clock".

Using the model-based clock requires you to specify the property, whose value will be used as the simulation time, as the value of a **timeValue**tag definition of a Simulation Config. You can specify the unit value for the simulation time through the **timeUnit** tag definition of the Simulation Configuration. If the **timeUnit** is unspecified, the millisecond will be used by default.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue setting for a model-based Clock.

When you select to use a model-based clock, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will ignore the clock ratio. The following are the constraints of the model-based clock

- There should be only one object of a classifier whose property is specified as timeValue. In the preceding figure, the property time of the block Clock has been set as the timeValue of the System Execution , which is a Simulation Config for simulating the System block. There should be only one Clock object in each simulation.
- You should not use a time event to update simulation time in a clock model. Because, it obtains simulation time from the model-based clock. In addition, a time event will be fired at a specific time or duration, and simulation time cannot be updated unless it is fired. Therefore, if you use a time event, simulation time will not be updated at all.

1350049294

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Internal simulation clock' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to use a value of any selected property to determine the simulation time. With this option, you can control the value of the simulation time with your model. The model that enables you to use the runtime value as the simulation time is called &quot;model-based clock&quot;. </p><p>Using the model-based clock requires you to specify the property, whose value will be used as the simulation time, as the value of a <strong>timeValue </strong>tag definition of a Simulation Config. If the <strong>timeUnit</strong> is unspecified, the millisecond will be used by default. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue Setting for a Model-based Clock" ac:alt="The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue Setting for a Model-based Clock"><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelbasedclock.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue setting for a model-based Clock.</h6><p>When you select to use a model-based clock, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will ignore the clock ratio. The following are the constraints of the model-based clock</p><ul><li><span>There should be only one object of a classifier whose property is specified as timeValue. In the preceding figure, the property time of the block </span><strong>Clock</strong><span> has been set as the timeValue of the </span><strong>System </strong><strong>Execution</strong><span>, which is a Simulation Config for simulating the System block. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571500 space=MMA version=3 -->
## PAGE 00660: Monte Carlo simulation

- page_id: `249571500`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571500/Monte+Carlo+simulation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:25.635+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Analysis pattern

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2145655573

INLINE

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces built-in support for Monte Carlo analysis, a technique that involves using random numbers and probability to solve problems. You can manage uncertainties and estimate how random parameters affect the overall performance of the system being modeled. Please refer to the [ATTACHMENT filename='HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.mdzip'] sample model on the welcome screen as the feature demonstration with the following steps.

##### Creating a system model

1. Transform the stochastic model: D > A+B+C (random components) to the deterministic model: D-(A+B+C) > 0 .
2. Create the system model and Parts with a Block Definition diagram with required value properties, e.g., Blocks Hinge , AB , C , and D .
3. Create constraint Blocks with parameters and constraint specification according to the requirement, e.g., Clearance_equation , R1 , and R2 .
4. Create a Requirement with a satisfy Relation to the value property to keep the result for the constraint, e.g., Hinge Clearance and Unassemblable .
5. Apply a «distribution» Type to get a set of random inputs of the value properties of the Parts based on Requirements, e.g., «uniform» with max and min and «normal» with mean and standardDeviation . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Create a parametric diagram in the system to bind the value properties to the parameters of the constraint Block, e.g., Clearance equation . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Inheriting the Hinge Analysis Block from the MonteCarloAnalysis Block

1. Create another Block Definition diagram and include an analysis context definition by dragging the MonteCarloAnalysis Block from MD Customization for SysML::analysis patterns into the newly created Block Definition diagram, e.g., AnalysisDefinition .
2. Create and inherit a new Block from the Block Definition diagram created in Step 1 as an analysis Block, e.g., Hinge Analysis , to get OutOfSpec , N , Mean , and Deviation properties. The inheritance can be done by creating a generalization Relation from the MonteCarloAnalysis Block to the system model Block (from the section).
3. Create an association Relation, e.g., hinge , to make the Hinge system model block part of the analysis Block, e.g., Hinge Analysis . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a parametric diagram and binding values

1. To specify a parameter as the target of Monte Carlo analysis, create a parametric diagram in the analysis block to bind properties and constraints. The statistical value will be recorded in Mean as a result of the termination of the analysis loop, e.g., clearance .
2. Connect constraints from Requirements to Mean for the Requirement verification and to OutOfSpec for the percentage of the samples whose Mean value violates any of the attached constraints or Requirements, e.g., R1 and R2 . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a Simulation Configuration diagram and configuring other settings

1. Create a Simulation Configuration diagram, add a SimulationConfig to the newly created diagram, and set the following tags:
  - executionTarget : the analysis block, e.g., Hinge Analysis Block .
  - numberOfRuns : the number of runs, e.g., 5000 .
  - resultLocation : an Instance table, e.g., Analysis results .
  - silent : true for the optimum performance of the simulation.
  - *name* (optional): e.g., *Monte Carlo Analysis*. NoteFor a model that has Behaviors (Classifier Behavior and/or Part Property with Behaviors), see the *autoStart* tag in [SimulationConfig](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/SimulationConfig+stereotype).If *numberOfRuns* of any table (*executionTarget*) is more than 1, it will be ignored in the Monte Carlo simulation. The table, however, will be run only once.If *silent* is set to false, the simulation will run with animation and idle time for each iteration, which is not practical for the Monte Carlo simulation.
2. Drag a *[Histogram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Histogram)* control from the Simulation toolbar to the Simulation Configuration diagram. You can use the histogram as a local user interface by setting the following tags:
  - represents : the analysis Block, e.g., Hinge Analysis .
  - value : the monitored value property, e.g., clearance .
  - dynamic : true for viewing dynamically updated statistical values ( false will open the histogram at the end of execution).
  - name (optional) : e.g., Histogram .
3. Record generated value properties of every iteration using the [CSV Export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/CSV+Export) control by setting the following tags:
  - represents : the system model Block, e.g., Hinge .
  - value : related value properties, e.g., a.width, b.width, or clearance .
  - fileName : the exported file name, e.g., clearance.csv .
  - name (optional): e.g., clearance .
4. Drag the Histogram and CSV Export controls to the «SimulationConfig». UI and executionListeners tags will be updated with the names of the Histogram and CSV Export controls accordingly, e.g., Histogram and clearance . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Running SimulationConfig and reviewing results

1. Run the SimulationConfig from the previous section, e.g., Monte Carlo Analysis .
2. During the simulation, the histogram will dynamically show the estimated distribution of the values of the analysis context definition at the top right, e.g., hinge.clearance, N, Mean, SD, and OutOfSpec .
3. The simulation progress bar will be shown with the number of iterations and time elapsed. You can click Cancel to terminate the simulation, and the analysis result will be saved at the terminated iteration. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. The summary result is recorded in the Instance table along with « VerificationStatus » between value property and constraint, e.g., Mean-R1 and OutOfSpec-R2 . You can also see the details of both failed and passed constraints in the tooltip when hovering the mouse over any highlighted values. [ATTACHMENT filename='analysis_results_in_instance_table.png']
5. Sampling results of value properties from applied [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distribution Extensions' space=''] are exported to the CSV file in the same location as the project. The file can be accessed through the link, e.g., clearance.csv , in the Console pane. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also see the details of both failed and passed constraints in the tooltip when hovering the mouse over any highlighted values.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="analysis_results_in_instance_table.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Sampling results of value properties from applied <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Distribution Extensions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[distributions stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are exported to the CSV file in the same location as the project. The file can be accessed through the link, e.g., <em><u>clearance.csv</u></em>, in the <strong>Console</strong> pane.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-8.PNG" /></ac:image></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570895 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00661: Nested Connector end

- page_id: `249570895`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570895/Nested+Connector+end
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:18.988+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['nested-connector-end']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the ends of a Connector that connects properties are stereotyped with «NestedConnectorEnd», Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the information from the propertyPath of the nested Connector end to find the right objects that specify the properties at both ends of the Connector. Therefore, you can send a Signal along the Connector to a deep nesting object. You can also use the binding Connector to bind objects that specify the properties at different nesting levels.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the ends of a Connector that connects properties are stereotyped with «NestedConnectorEnd», Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the information from the propertyPath of the nested Connector end to find the right objects that specify the properties at both ends of the Connector. Therefore, you can send a Signal along the Connector to a deep nesting object. You can also use the binding Connector to bind objects that specify the properties at different nesting levels. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249569885 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00662: Nested property selection for configurations

- page_id: `249569885`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249569885/Nested+property+selection+for+configurations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:07.798+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['nested-properties', 'selecting-nested-properties-for-configurations']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit, from version 18.4, includes support for nested property selection for configurations, such as Time series charts, Timelines, Sequence diagram generator, and CSV export.

The Specification window for the aforementioned items will have the following two properties

- **Represents** Specifies the Classifier represented by the Configuration.
- Value The Value property takes nested property values.

The **Select Nested Properties** dialog will open if you click the button beside the Value property from the Specification window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Select Nested Properties dialog.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

Before the **Value** property can be specified, the **Represents** property has to be assigned a value first.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit, from version 18.4, includes support for nested property selection for configurations, such as Time series charts, Timelines</span><span style="color: black;">, Sequence diagram generator, and CSV export.</span></p><p class="Textbody"><span style="color: black;">The Specification window for the aforementioned items will have the following two properties<br /></span></p><ul><li class="Textbody"><span style="color: black;"><strong>Represents<br /></strong></span><span>Specifies the Classifier represented by the Configuration.</span></li><li class="Textbody"><strong style="color: black;line-height: 1.42857;">Value</strong><span style="color: black;"> <br />The Value property takes nested property values.</span></li></ul><p class="Textbody"><span style="color: black;">The <strong>Select Nested Properties</strong> dialog will open if you click the button beside the Value property from the Specification window.  <br /> </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: black;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Select Nested Properties Dialog" ac:alt="Select Nested Properties Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested-properties-dialog-a.png" /></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Select Nested Properties dialog.</span></span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e351f37c-e660-4f44-af9a-ecb849208f4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Before the <strong>Value</strong> property can be specified, the <strong>Represents</strong> property has to be assigned a value first.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249569841 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00663: Nested UI Configuration stereotype

- page_id: `249569841`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249569841/Nested+UI+Configuration+stereotype
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:07.620+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['nested-ui-configuration-stereotype']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1422162079

1422162082

1422162080

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit provides a «NestedUIConfig» stereotype to create a complex UI mockup, which consists of multiple UI Configs. This stereotype contains two tag definitions: (i) feature and (ii) config. The feature tag is mandatory. It specifies which Property (part) of the context it will represent. The Config tag specifies which existing UI configuration will be used and displayed as the UI of the system part, which is represented by the Property specified in the feature tag. A Nested UI Configuration can also show images of UI that is defined as the tagged value of Config tag definition, such as Panel, activeImage, and Time series chart.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Images of Nested User Interface Configuration.

You can use a NestedUIConfig stereotype to either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Representing parts of a simulation context' space=''] (This part can nest components that represent the nested properties) or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Representing parts using existing UI Configurations' space='']. The NestedUIConfig stereotype can be applied with the UI Panel or UI Group Box. When it is applied and its tag definitions are set, it can be represented as a part of its owner component.

1422162078

**Related pages**

This stereotype contains two tag definitions: (i) feature and (ii) config. The feature tag is mandatory. It specifies which Property (part) of the context it will represent. The Config tag specifies which existing UI configuration will be used and displayed as the UI of the system part, which is represented by the Property specified in the feature tag. A Nested UI Configuration can also show images of UI that is defined as the tagged value of Config tag definition, such as Panel, activeImage, and Time series chart.</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Images of Nested User Interface Configuration" ac:alt="Images of Nested User Interface Configuration"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NestedConfigShowingImages2.PNG" /></ac:image><br />Images of Nested User Interface Configuration.</h6><p>You can use a NestedUIConfig stereotype to either <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Representing parts of a simulation context" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[represent a part of a simulation context]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (This part can nest components that represent the nested properties) or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Representing parts using existing UI Configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[representing a part using existing UI Configurations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The NestedUIConfig stereotype can be applied with the UI Panel or UI Group Box. When it is applied and its tag definitions are set, it can be represented as a part of its owner component.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3d3f885-d7ea-4cc0-8051-eab8d2d06aa6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422162078</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b644387e-6f6b-4afa-aef2-28ab32dc5ebb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570699 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00664: Object binding

- page_id: `249570699`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570699/Object+binding
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-02T10:45:29.435+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Parametric evaluator > Value binding
- labels: ['object-binding']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a binding connector connects properties that is typed by either a class or a block, the runtime value that specifies each property will be the referent of the same block object. Since you can use only a SysML binding connector to bind objects, the SysML plugin is required.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If a binding connector connects properties that is typed by either a class or a block, the runtime value that specifies each property will be the referent of the same block object. Since you can use only a SysML binding connector to bind objects, the SysML plugin is required. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector" ac:alt="Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="objectBinding.png" /></ac:image><br />Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570055 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00665: Opening a diagram of an executing behavior

- page_id: `249570055`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570055/Opening+a+diagram+of+an+executing+behavior
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:08.461+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Animation
- labels: ['opening-executing-behavior-diagram', 'diagram-of-executing-behavior']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you simulate a model, the **Sessions** pane will open to display simulation of each element in the model. During the simulation, if you ever need to see what diagram a Behavior being simulated has, you can double-click the session in the **Sessions** pane of the **Simulation** window. The following figure shows the **Sessions** pane when Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is executing the **Blackjack** sample (*Blackjack.mdzip*). If you double-click the Behaviors outlined in red, a new tab will open to display the corresponding diagram.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Sessions tab of the Simulation window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you simulate a model, the <strong>Sessions</strong> pane will open to display simulation of each element in the model. During the simulation, if you ever need to see what diagram a Behavior being simulated has, you can double-click the session in the <strong>Sessions</strong> pane of the <strong>Simulation</strong> window. The following figure shows the <strong>Sessions</strong> pane when Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is executing the <strong>Blackjack</strong> sample (<em>Blackjack.mdzip</em>). If you double-click the Behaviors outlined in red, a new tab will open to display the corresponding diagram. </p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Sessions Tab of the Simulation Window" ac:alt="The Sessions Tab of the Simulation Window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sessionsTab.png" /></ac:image><br />The Sessions tab of the Simulation window.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570770 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00666: Operators

- page_id: `249570770`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570770/Operators
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-09-03T13:23:19.779+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Parametric evaluator > Built-in Math
- labels: ['operators']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Important

Important

• **x**and **y** represent numerical values or variables. 
• **m**, **n**, and **p**represent integer values or variables. 
• **a**and **b** represent boolean values or variables. 
• **U**and **V**represent matrices of numerical values. 
• **A**and **B**represent matrices of boolean values.

#### Arithmetic operators

| Operator | Operator name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| + | Addition | x+yU + V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| - | Subtraction | x-yU + V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| * | Multiplication | x*yU*V (U is an m x n matrix and V is an n x p matrix) |
| / | Division | x/y |
| % | Modulus | m%nU + V (U and V are m x n matrices of integer values).This operator operates element-wise on matrices. |
| ! | Factorial | m! |
| ^ | Power | x^y |
| \ | Left division | x\y is equivalent to (1/x) * yU \ V (U and V are m x n matrices) is equivalent to (1/U) * V |
| .* | Element-wise multiplication | U .* V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| ./ | Element-wise division | U ./ V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| .\ | Element-wise left division | U .\ V (U and V are m x n matrices) is equivalent to (1/U) .* V |
| .^ | Element-wise power | U .^ V (U and V are m x n matrices) |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An Element-wise operator performs an operation on each pair of Elements, which is in the same location, of the operand matrices.

#### Assignment operators

| Operator | Operator name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| = | Assignment | x=ya=bU=V |

#### Comparison operators

| Operator | Operator name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| > | Greater | x>yU>V |
| < | Less | x<yU<V |
| >= | Greater or Equal | x>=yU>=V |
| <= | Less of Equal | x<=yU<=V |
| == | Equality | x==ya==bU==V |
| != | Inequality | x!=ya!=bU!=V |

All comparison operators operate Element-wise on matrices in the example as follows

A = [1; 2; 3] 
B = [3; 2; 1]

Then 
A>B is [false; false; true];

#### Boolean operators

| Operator | Operator Name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ! NOTnot | NOT | !ANOT Anot A |
| &ANDand | AND | A&BA AND BA and B |
| \|ORor | OR | A\|BA OR BA or B |
| ^XORxor | XOR (exclusive OR) | A^BA XOR BA xor B |

#### NOTE: Important

Important

All boolean operators operate element-wise on matrices in the example as follows

A = [true; true; false; false]; 
B = [true; false; true; false];

Then 
A&B is [true; false; false; false];

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571539 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00667: Parameter sweep

- page_id: `249571539`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571539/Parameter+sweep
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-13T07:51:04.882+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Analysis pattern > Trade study analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

One of the ways of specifying alternatives for trade study analysis is Parameter Sweep. Parameter Sweep generates alternatives automatically based on the specified parameters. After you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Trade study analysis' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Trade study analysis' space=''] for this Block, specify the ranges for design variables using one of four available methods described below.

TradeStudyExamples

install_root

##### Specifying ranges by step

If you specify ranges for design variables by using this method, values of a specific property are generated according to the specified step. There are two possible outcomes:

- If you specify a positive step (step > 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified minimum value and then increased by the specified step until the specified maximum value is reached.
- If you specify a negative step (step < 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified maximum value and then decreased by the specified step until the specified minimum value is reached.

To specify ranges for design variables by step

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the values of the **Min**, **Max**, and **Step** properties. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ExamplesIf specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=5, and **Step**=1, the generated values are 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.If specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=2.5, and **Step**=-0.4, the generated values are 2.5, 2.1, 1.7, and 1.3.If specified ranges are **Max**=10 and **Step**=4, the generated values are 0, 4, and 8. (If the **Min** property value is not specified, the default value is 0.)

##### Specifying ranges by a number of points

If you specify ranges for design variables by a number of points, the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified minimum value and then increased by a calculated step according to the specified number of points until the specified maximum value is reached.****

To specify ranges for design variables by a number of points

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the values of the **Min**, **Max**, and **Number Of Points** properties. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ExamplesIf specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=5, and **Number Of Points**=3, a calculated step is 2 and generated values are 1, 3, and 5.If specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=2.5, and **Number Of Points**=6, a calculated step is 0.3 and generated values are 1, 1.3, 1.6, 1.9, 2.2, and 2.5.If specified ranges are **Min**=-15, **Max**=0, and **Number Of Points**=4, a calculated step is 5 and generated values are -15, -10, -5, and 0.

##### Specifying ranges by a number of points and step

If you specify ranges for design variables by using this method, the values of a specific property are generated according to the specified step and number of points. There are two possible outcomes:

- If you specify a positive step (step > 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified minimum value and then increased by the specified step until the number of generated values reaches the specified number of point or maximum value.
- If you specify a negative step (step < 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified maximum value and then decreased by the specified step until the number of generated values reaches the specified number of points or minimum value.

To specify ranges for design variables by a number of points and step

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the values of the **Min**, **Max**, **Number Of Points,**and**Step** properties. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ExamplesIf specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Number Of Points**=5, and **Step**=1, the generated values are 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.If specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=4, **Number Of Points**=3, and **Step**=1.8, the generated values are 1 and 2.8. (The value of 4.6 is excluded because it is greater than the maximum value of 4.)If specified ranges are **Max**=1, **Number Of Points**=3, and **Step**=-0.1, the generated values are 1, 0.9, and 0.8.

##### Specifying ranges by a value list

If you specify ranges for design variables by a value list, a set of specified values is used for a specific property.

To specify ranges for design variables by a value list

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the value of the **Values** property. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If the Values property is specified together with the Step , N umber Of Points , Min , or Max properties, only the Values property will be used in parameter sweep.
- If the Min property value is not specified, the default value of 0 (zero) is used instead.

##### ****Error handling

- WARN: Since the minimal value of the [value name] design variable is not specified, the default value of 0 is used instead. The warning is shown when the Max property value is not specified in these combinations: [ Min , Max , Step ] or [ Min , Max , Number Of Points ], Step <0. Invalid values shall be excluded from the trade study analysis.
- WARN: The maximal value of the [value name] design variable is not specified. If the Number Of Points property value is 1, then the Min property value is taken as an alternative result (calculating the step is not required). In addition, the warning is shown on the console.
- WARN: Since the Number Of Points value of the [value name] design variable is equal to 1, the minimum value is used instead. The invalid values are excluded from the trade study analysis, and a generic warning (The [value name] design variable is specified incorrectly.) is shown on the console when:
  - The Step property is specified in real numbers (e.g., 1.5; 0.25) but the value is typed as an integer. Exception : the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 5.0).
  - The Step is calculated in real numbers, but the value is typed as an integer (e.g. Min =1, Max =10, Number Of Points =10, calculated Step =0.11). Exception : the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 2.0).
  - Neither the Step nor Number Of Points property value is specified.
  - Step =0 Exception : the warning is not displayed if Number Of Points >0.
  - Number Of Points <=0
  - Min >= Max Exception : the warning is not displayed if Number Of Points =1.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

8. (If the <strong>Min</strong> property value is not specified, the default value is 0.)</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><br />Specifying ranges by a number of points</h3><p>If you specify ranges for design variables by a number of points, the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified relevant property.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the values of the <strong>Min</strong>, <strong>Max</strong>, <strong>Number Of Points, </strong>and<strong> Step</strong> properties.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="number_of_points_and_step.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0ec822e-ec4e-49d3-84ed-fb9d1dfc8ddd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If specified ranges are <strong>Min</strong>=1, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=5, and <strong>Step</strong>=1, the generated values are 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.</li><li>If specified ranges are <strong>Min</strong>=1, <strong>Max</strong>=4, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=3, and <strong>Step</strong>=1.8, the generated values are 1 and 2.8. (The value of 4.6 is excluded because it is greater than the maximum value of 4.)</li><li>If specified ranges are <strong>Max</strong>=1, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=3, and <strong>Step</strong>=-0.1, the generated values are 1, 0.9, and 0.8.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><br />Specifying ranges by a value list</h3><p>If you specify ranges for design variables by a value list, a set of specified values is used for a specific property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify ranges for design variables by a value list</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the value of the <strong>Values</strong> property.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="value_list.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23f74907-3607-461a-844e-c05b43bb4eaa"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the <strong>Values</strong> property is specified together with the <strong>Step</strong>, <strong>N</strong><strong>umber Of Points</strong>, <strong>Min</strong>, or <strong>Max</strong> properties, only the <strong>Values</strong> property will be used in parameter sweep.</li><li>If the <strong>Min</strong> property value is not specified, the default value of 0 (zero) is used instead.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong><br /></strong>Error handling</h3><ul><li>WARN: Since the minimal value of the <ins><span class="error">[value name]</span></ins> design variable is not specified, the default value of 0 is used instead.<br />The warning is shown when the <strong>Max</strong> property value is not specified in these combinations: [<strong>Min</strong>, <strong>Max</strong>, <strong>Step</strong>] or [<strong>Min</strong>, <strong>Max</strong>, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>], <strong>Step</strong>&lt;0. Invalid values shall be excluded from the trade study analysis.<br /><br /></li><li>WARN: The maximal value of the <ins>[value name]</ins> design variable is not specified.<br />If the <strong>Number Of Points</strong> property value is 1, then the <strong>Min</strong> property value is taken as an alternative result (calculating the step is not required). In addition, the warning is shown on the console.<br /><br /></li><li>WARN: Since the Number Of Points value of the <ins><span class="error">[value name]</span></ins> design variable is equal to 1, the minimum value is used instead.<br />The invalid values are excluded from the trade study analysis, and a generic warning (The <ins><span class="error">[value name]</span></ins> design variable is specified incorrectly.) is shown on the console when:<br /><ul><li>The <strong>Step</strong> property is specified in real numbers (e.g., 1.5; 0.25) but the value is typed as an integer.<br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 5.0).</li><li>The <strong>Step</strong> is calculated in real numbers, but the value is typed as an integer (e.g. <strong>Min</strong>=1, <strong>Max</strong>=10, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=10, calculated <strong>Step</strong>=0.11).<br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 2.0).</li><li>Neither the <strong>Step</strong> nor <strong>Number Of Points</strong> property value is specified.</li><li><strong>Step</strong>=0<br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if <strong>Number Of Points</strong>&gt;0.</li><li><strong>Number Of Points</strong>&lt;=0</li><li><strong>Min</strong>&gt;=<strong>Max</strong><br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=1.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570747 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00668: Parametric debugging

- page_id: `249570747`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570747/Parametric+debugging
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:51:00.126+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Parametric evaluator
- labels: ['parametric-animation', 'animating-parametrics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Simulation Toolkit allows debugging the Parametric diagram through [Breakpoints](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Breakpoints). It is especially helpful when working on projects with a large number of elements or parametric patterns that are applied recursively. Because the Parametric diagram usually runs at the maximum speed (without delay) regardless of the **Animation Speed** settings (see also [SimulationConfig](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/SimulationConfig+stereotype)), you must set Breakpoints to monitor values.

In the figure below, Simulation pauses at the *margin* breakpoint in the Parametric diagram in which you can debug the model. You can also find the sample model of parametric debugging on the Welcome screen by going to the **Simulation** sample group and selecting **SpacecraftMassRollup**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Debugging parameters of the SpacecraftMassRollup built-in sample.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Simulation Toolkit allows debugging the Parametric diagram through <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Breakpoints">Breakpoints</a>. It is especially helpful when working on projects with a large number of elements or parametric patterns that are applied recursively. Because the Parametric diagram usually runs at the maximum speed (without delay) regardless of the <strong>Animation Speed</strong> settings (see also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a>), you must set Breakpoints to monitor values.</p><p>In the figure below, Simulation pauses at the <em>margin</em> breakpoint in the Parametric diagram in which you can debug the model. You can also find the sample model of parametric debugging on the Welcome screen by going to the <strong>Simulation</strong> sample group and selecting <strong>SpacecraftMassRollup</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Debugging a Parametric of the Built-in Sample.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Debugging parameters of the SpacecraftMassRollup built-in sample.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570679 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00669: Parametric evaluator

- page_id: `249570679`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570679/Parametric+evaluator
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:14.573+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide
- labels: ['parametric-evaluator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit comes with the parametric evaluator to help you solve constraint expressions in your model. The parametric evaluator is designed to work with the SysML Parametric Diagram. But, you can also use it to solve constraints on any UML classes. With the parametric evaluator, you can define a mathematical or a logical expression as a constraint on a block or a class to limit the values of its properties. If the expression is an equation, the parametric evaluator will evaluate the expression of the constraint and update the values of the properties with the result of the evaluation. If the expression is a logical expression, the parametric evaluator will use the expression to validate the values of the properties.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit comes with the parametric evaluator to help you solve constraint expressions in your model. The parametric evaluator is designed to work with the SysML Parametric Diagram. But, you can also use it to solve constraints on any UML classes. With the parametric evaluator, you can define a mathematical or a logical expression as a constraint on a block or a class to limit the values of its properties. If the expression is an equation, the parametric evaluator will evaluate the expression of the constraint and update the values of the properties with the result of the evaluation. If the expression is a logical expression, the parametric evaluator will use the expression to validate the values of the properties. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570881 space=MMA version=3 -->
## PAGE 00670: Port

- page_id: `249570881`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570881/Port
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:18.317+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

944122966

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

944122979

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

944122968

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the following SysML ports:

- Flow Port A Port stereotyped with «FlowPort» that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment.
- Full Port A Port stereotyped with «FullPort» that specifies a separate element of an owning Block.
- Proxy Port A Port stereotyped with «ProxyPort» that the value specifying the Proxy Port is the reference of the object that is the target of the Flow.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- A Conjugated Port (isConjugated = True) is indicated with a tilde (∼) in front of the Port type, showing that the property Flow direction of the Port is reversed, from **in** to **out** or from **out** to **in**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']A conjugated Port with a tilde (∼) in front of the Port type.
- For the nested Port, if the parent Port is conjugated, all of the property Flow directions of its children and grandchildren will be reversed.
- A Behavior Port is supported during simulation. If a port is a behavior port (isBehavior = True), it will pass the arriving requests directly to the owning classifier while ignoring all delegation connectors. [ATTACHMENT filename='behavior_port.png']

###### Behavior Port

944122965

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570696 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00671: Primitive value binding

- page_id: `249570696`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570696/Primitive+value+binding
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:14.873+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Parametric evaluator > Value binding
- labels: ['primitive-value-binding']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Primitive value binding connects two value properties or properties that are typed by a primitive type so that whenever the value of one property changes, the value of the other property will also change. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit maintains the values of those connected properties to be the same at both ends. Changing the value at one end causes Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to update the value of the property at the other end of the connector. The figures below show the primitive value binding with a SysML binding connector and a UML binding connector respectively.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing the execution of blocks. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing the results of a simulation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Primitive value binding connects two value properties or properties that are typed by a primitive type so that whenever the value of one property changes, the value of the other property will also change. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit maintains the values of those connected properties to be the same at both ends. Changing the value at one end causes Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to update the value of the property at the other end of the connector. The figures below show the primitive value binding with a SysML binding connector and a UML binding connector respectively. </p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Primitive Value Binding with a SysML Binding Connector" ac:alt="Primitive Value Binding with a SysML Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="primValuebinding_sysml.png" /></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing the execution of blocks.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:title="Primitive Value Binding with a UML Binding Connector" ac:alt="Primitive Value Binding with a UML Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="primValuebinding_uml.png" /></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing the results of a simulation.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570169 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00672: Printing constraint failures in the console

- page_id: `249570169`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570169/Printing+constraint+failures+in+the+console
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:09.144+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation debugging > Simulation console
- labels: ['constraint-failures', 'error-messages-about-constraint-failures']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33730985

33730988

33730986

Most of model simulations involve with evaluating values that are in the model. Evaluation will fail if the model cannot satisfy any of the constraints. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit acknowledges this kind of failure as a constraint failure and thus will print the error message in the **Console** pane. The message will inform you about the unsatisfied constraint(s) including hyperlinks that help you navigate the failed requirements and constraint(s), and the diagram where the simulation stopped.

The following figure shows an example of constraint failures printed on the **Console** pane

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Printing constraint failures in the Console pane.

33730984

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Console pane' space='']

Evaluation will fail if the model cannot satisfy any of the constraints. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit acknowledges this kind of failure as a constraint failure and thus will print the error message in the <strong>Console</strong> pane. The message will inform you about the unsatisfied constraint(s) including hyperlinks that help you navigate the failed requirements and constraint(s), and the diagram where the simulation stopped. </p><p>The following figure shows an example of constraint failures printed on the <strong>Console</strong> pane</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Printing Constraint Failures in the Console Pane" ac:alt="Printing Constraint Failures in the Console Pane"><ri:attachment ri:filename="110073_ConstraintFail.png" /></ac:image><br />Printing constraint failures in the Console pane.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7efd82d8-72d0-46d1-b2aa-5e4b85e54b65"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33730984</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Console pane" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570896 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00673: Probability

- page_id: `249570896`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570896/Probability
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:19.125+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['probability']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Probability is a stereotype in SysML. You can apply it to outgoing edges of decision nodes and object nodes. When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a decision node or an object nodes whose outgoing edges stereotyped with probability, it will use the probability values collected from all outgoing edges to select one outgoing edge that it will go to.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges.

Normally, the summation of the probability values from all outgoing edges should be 1.0 (100%). If this is not the case, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will scale the probability values with the summation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Probability is a stereotype in SysML. You can apply it to outgoing edges of decision nodes and object nodes. When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a decision node or an object nodes whose outgoing edges stereotyped with probability, it will use the probability values collected from all outgoing edges to select one outgoing edge that it will go to. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges" ac:alt="Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ProbabilitySupported.png" /></ac:image><br />Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges.</h6><p>Normally, the summation of the probability values from all outgoing edges should be 1.0 (100%). If this is not the case, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will scale the probability values with the summation. </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249569658 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00674: Project options

- page_id: `249569658`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249569658/Project+options
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:50:56.922+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['project-options', 'simulation-project-options']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize a simulation project, e.g., animation, simulation framework, and simulation engines through project options. When you save the project, those project options will also be saved. In addition, you can restore those options to default values with the **Reset to Defaults** button.

To customize project options

1. Open a simulation project.
2. On the main menu, click Options and select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. On the left pane, click General > Simulation .

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The **Options** > **Project****command will be available only if one or more projects are open.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Simulation Project Options dialog.

Groups of the project options are as follows:

- Animation Customize animations of the simulation: colors of annotated elements, auto open diagrams, and silent options. See also customizing animation .
- Simulation Framework Customize general Behaviors of the simulation. See also validation and verification and integration with external Evaluators .
- Sequence Diagram Generator Record the Sequence diagram generator. See also recording simulation as a Sequence diagram .
- fUML Engine Customize Behaviors of Activity simulation. See also Activity simulation engine .
- Parametric Evaluator Customize Behaviors of Parametric simulation. See also integration with external Evaluators and specifying the language for the expression .
- SCXML Engine Customize Behaviors of State Machine simulation. See also completion Events and Transitions and State activation semantics .
- Simulation Script Engine Select JAR file(s) and load them to the script engine.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can customize a simulation project, e.g., animation, simulation framework, and simulation engines through project options. When you save the project, those project options will also be saved. In addition, you can restore those options to default values with the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button.</p><p>To customize project options</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a simulation project.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> and select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>On the left pane, click <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Simulation</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8a6fd10-9ccc-4560-af2f-f3757ff64962"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong><em> </em>command will be available only if one or more projects are open.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="All-Project Options.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ab6b547-4c31-40e8-98e2-d3cf19ac031c">The Simulation Project Options dialog.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><p>Groups of the project options are as follows:</p><ul><li><strong>Animation</strong><br />Customize animations of the simulation: colors of annotated elements, auto open diagrams, and silent options. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Customizing+animation">customizing animation</a>.</li><li><strong>Simulation Framework</strong><br />Customize general Behaviors of the simulation. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Validation+and+verification">validation and verification</a> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Integration+with+external+Evaluators">integration with external Evaluators</a>.</li><li><strong>Sequence Diagram Generator</strong><br />Record the Sequence diagram generator. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Recording+simulation+as+a+Sequence+diagram">recording simulation as a Sequence diagram</a>.</li><li><strong>fUML Engine</strong><br />Customize Behaviors of Activity simulation. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Activity+simulation+engine">Activity simulation engine</a>.</li><li><strong>Parametric Evaluator</strong><br />Customize Behaviors of Parametric simulation. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Integration+with+external+Evaluators">integration with external Evaluators</a> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Specifying+the+language+for+the+expression">specifying the language for the expression</a>.</li><li><strong>SCXML Engine</strong><br />Customize Behaviors of State Machine simulation. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Completion+Events+and+Transitions">completion Events and Transitions</a> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/State+activation+semantics">State activation semantics</a>.</li><li><strong>Simulation Script Engine</strong><br />Select JAR file(s) and load them to the script engine.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570889 space=MMA version=3 -->
## PAGE 00675: Proxy Port

- page_id: `249570889`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570889/Proxy+Port
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:18.644+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements > Port
- labels: ['proxy-port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

944183898

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

944183910

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

944183900

A Proxy Port is a Port stereotyped with «ProxyPort». When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates an object that has a proxy Port, the value that specifies the proxy Port will be the reference of the object that is the target of the Flow. A Classifier of the target object must be inherited from an interface Block that types a proxy Port.

If the proxy Port is not connected by a delegation Connector to the internal structure of the owning object, the target will be the object itself. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The target object owning object of the proxy Port.

If the proxy Port is connected by a delegation Connector to the internal structure of the object owning the Port, the target of the Flow will be the object specifying the role at the other end of the delegation Connector. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The target Objects of proxy Ports are visible when the Ports connected by Delegation Connector.

If Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit cannot find the target object, or the classifier of the target object does not inherit from the interface Block that types the proxy Port, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will initialize the Port object directly from its type.

Features defined by Interface Block, e.g., Flow properties, can be displayed in the **Variables** pane (when selecting the **Show Ports** option for the **Variables** pane in the **Project Options** dialog) under Proxy Port nodes during simulation as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Proxy Port nodes (level and valve) show only features defined by «interfaceBlock» (level and valve Flow properties).

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Other properties which are not part of the Ports, e.g., *minlevel* and *maxlevel* of *Controller* are not shown because Ports can be only accessed and display what is defined by «interfaceBlock».

944183896

**Related pages**

- Port
- Flow Port
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571008 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00676: Reading enumeration literal value

- page_id: `249571008`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571008/Reading+enumeration+literal+value
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:20.795+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Action languages
- labels: ['enumeration-literal-value']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can evaluate any expression that contains enumeration literal by name directly, e.g., colour == "Green", but not colour.getName() == Green.

The following figure demonstrates how you can read Enumeration literal value by name directly through the property **Change Expression** colour=="Green" and setting the **guard condition** colour=="Red" instead of colour.getName()=="Green", and colour.getName()=="Red" accordingly in the Specification window of the Transition<>.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reading Enumeration literal value by name directly (without .getName()).

Backward compatibility with earlier version models using getName() is supported through an error message in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation console' space=''] pane. The Script engine will throw one error message, and then stop for Enumeration literal value. If getName() exists in many places, you will have to run the simulation many times and manually fix it one by one.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Standard">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can evaluate any expression that contains enumeration literal by name directly, e.g., colour == &quot;Green&quot;, but not colour.getName() == Green.</p><p class="Standard">The following figure demonstrates how you can read Enumeration literal value by name directly through the property <strong>Change Expression</strong> colour==&quot;Green&quot; and setting the <strong>guard condition</strong> colour==&quot;Red&quot; instead of colour.getName()==&quot;Green&quot;, and colour.getName()==&quot;Red&quot; accordingly in the Specification window of the Transition&lt;&gt;.</p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enum_value.png" /></ac:image> </p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Reading Enumeration literal value by name directly (without .getName()). </h6><p class="Standard">Backward compatibility with earlier version models using getName() is supported through an error message in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f4a377cf-5d6a-4b4f-b1e8-6dbc7ee22ab8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Simulation console" /></ac:link> pane</ac:inline-comment-marker>. The Script engine will throw one error message, and then stop for Enumeration literal value. If getName() exists in many places, you will have to run the simulation many times and manually fix it one by one.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571081 space=MMA version=3 -->
## PAGE 00677: Reading runtime values from an Excel spreadsheet

- page_id: `249571081`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571081/Reading+runtime+values+from+an+Excel+spreadsheet
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:08:38.765+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Action languages > Action scripts APIs > Excel Helper APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit helps you read fUML runtime values from an Excel file. The following API will read the values of a specific row from a spreadsheet in an Excel file, and then create an fUML ValueList to store the values.

```text

```

The APIs can ignore the sheetName and use the first sheet of the selected spreadsheet instead.

```text

```

The following API will read the values from multiple rows of the selected spreadsheet in the Excel file, and then create the fUML ValueList to store the values.

```text

```

The APIs can ignore the sheetName and use the first sheet of the selected spreadsheet instead.

```text

```

If you want to read all values from the selected spreadsheet, you can use the following API:

```text

```

The API can ignore the sheetName and use the first sheet of the selected spreadsheet instead.

```text

```

The mappingElement parameter can be a String or an element for representing a mapping Class name or a mapping Class element.

#### INFO: Information

Information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MT']«ImportMap»can be used as the mappingElement. However, it is strongly recommended that you define the distinctive name of «ImportMap» and the «DiagramTableMapToDataSource» (an Instance table linked to an Excel file) when using to avoid conflict of object mappingElement.

The APIs can ignore the mappingElement then create the runtime object of the file schema element that is matched with filename.

```text

```

The following API can assign a name parameter to check the name of a mappingElement or a sheetName. If the matching mappingElement cannot be found, this API will use the file schema element to create a runtime object and check the sheet name of the Excel file with the name parameter.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
