The SysML Parametric diagrams and InstanceSpecifications are as follows</p><ul><li>simple_parametrics.mdzip: basic Parametric Simulation.</li><li>The CylinderPipe.mdzip sample: demonstrates how to deal with multiple values. It shows the calculation for the cost of raw materials that will be used to manufacture the cylinder pipes. It also   demonstrates the use of OpaqueBehaviorAction to simulate the parametric.</li><li>The ActParIntegrate.mdzip sample: demonstrates the use of OpaqueBehavior to simulate the parametric.</li><li>SCARA manipulator.mdzip: demonstrate the use of Parametric Simulation to evaluate the position of end-effector of the SCARA manipulator from the given angles of actuators.</li><li>MotionAnalysis.mdzip and SpringDisplacementUsingTimevariable.mdzip: show how to use Time Series Chart for plotting the runtime values.</li><li>Trade-Study for Brayton Cycle.mdzip: this sample shows you how to perform a trade-study.</li><li>Forward Contract Valuation.mdzip: a dynamic constraint usage in Parametric Simulation. </li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4902bc5b-756f-48f0-8c67-678efba327e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>All of the sample projects of the Parametric Simulation engine include a Simulation Configurations package that contains two SimulationConfig elements for normal and silent simulation. You can select this SimulationConfig class to start the Parametric Simulation engine. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Specifying+the+language+for+the+expression">Specifying the language for the expression</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Value+binding">Value binding</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Evaluating+expressions">Evaluating expressions</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Dynamic+constraint">Dynamic constraint</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Manual+value+updates+using+the+Parametric+Evaluator">Manual value updates using the Parametric Evaluator</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Communicating+with+evaluators+through+simulation+console">Communicating with evaluators through simulation console</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Built-in+Math">Built-in Math</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Integration+with+external+Evaluators">Integration with external Evaluators</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Trade study analysis" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Sample+project" class="current">Sample project</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570392 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00700: Sample projects

- page_id: `249570392`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570392/Sample+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-12-29T08:24:02.116+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > State Machine simulation
- labels: ['sample-projects', 'state-machine-simulation-samples']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

103619828

103619839

103619826

**On this page**

44

103619829

The State Machine simulation sample projects are available in the built-in **Small Tests**samples, in *<modeling_tool_installation_directory>\samples\simulation\SmallTestSamples.mdzip* or from the Welcome screen under the **Simulation** group of the modeling tool. In this topic, the focus is on the **State Tests** section for the State Machine simulation sample projects as follows:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The State Tests section of the Small Tests samples.

- Do Activity in State This test is designed to demonstrate how to use the Do Behavior in model execution. Run the **TestState_DoActivity** simulation configuration to see how it works. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Do Activity in State sample.
- Guard This test is designed to demonstrate how to use Guards on Transitions in model execution. Run the **TestState_Guard** simulation configuration to see how it works. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Guard sample.
- History This test is designed to demonstrate how to use the Composite State with Shallow History and Deep History in model execution. Click the **Run** button on the diagram toolbar to see how it works. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The History sample.
- Regions This test is designed to demonstrate how to use the Orthogonal State with Parallel Regions and Entry and Exit Activities in model execution. The Entry Activity is executed immediately after the State Machine is activated before any States in the inner regions. Next, all Initial States in the regions are activated simultaneously. The **Regions** sample demonstrates that multiple active States are executed at the same time. The Event list in the Simulation Console contains Triggers of all outgoing Transitions of all active States. If one of the parent States’ outgoing Transitions is triggered, the Exit Activity will be executed before the State Machine is deactivated. Click the **Run with Context** button on the diagram toolbar to see how it works. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Regions sample.
- Timing Events This test is designed to demonstrate how to use timing Events in model execution. Both relative time and absolute time are supported (**Is Relative=true/false**). Click the **Run** button on the diagram toolbar to see how it works. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Timing Events sample.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571185 space=MMA version=5 -->
## PAGE 00701: Sample projects for simulation with Jupyter Notebook

- page_id: `249571185`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571185/Sample+projects+for+simulation+with+Jupyter+Notebook
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-03T09:43:49.711+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Server-side simulation > Simulation using Jupyter Notebook
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you set up Jupyter Notebook, you can open and test sample projects created for server-side simulation using Jupyter. This chapter briefly describes the sample projects and explains how to execute them.

To access the sample projects

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation using Jupyter Notebook' space=''] if you have not already done so.
2. Extract the < install_root >\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation\pyST.zip file. The sample projects are located in the samples directory.

##### Bouncing Ball sample

The *Bouncing Ball* sample project demonstrates server-side simulation with FMU. In addition, you can draw a chart based on the CSV data after the model execution.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Add the Bouncing Ball sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.

To run the *Bouncing Ball* sample and draw a chart based on the CSV data

1. Run the *Bouncing Ball* project and get the CSV data. Request example for running the simulation
2. Draw a chart based on the CSV data. Request example for drawing a chart based on the CSV data

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Bouncing Ball* chart drawn based on the CSV data.

##### Coffee Machine sample

The *Coffee Machine* sample project demonstrates server-side simulation with a specified HTML UI mockup. With this project, you can open the HTML UI window during model execution.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- Add the Coffee Machine sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.
- To open the HTML UI during server-side simulation, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auto-generating HTML files' space=''] and commit the changes to the server .

To run the *Coffee Machine* sample and open the HTML UI window

1. Run the *Coffee Machine* project. Request example for running the simulation
2. Check the simulation status. Request example for getting the simulation status/simulation/api/ui/20a84d37-0aba-4e12-9f14-ca381a5952f4/CoffeeMachine.html'],
 'project': 'CoffeeMachine',
 'config': 'Coffee Machine Web',
 'elapsedTime': 72742}]]>
3. Click the UI URL in the response to the status request to open the HTML UI window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The HTML UI window opened during the *Coffee Machine* project execution.

##### Spacecraft Mass Rollup sample

The *Spacecraft Mass Rollup* sample demonstrates server-side simulation with specified input and output parameters.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Add the Spacecraft Mass Rollup sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.

To run the *Spacecraft Mass Rollup* sample with input and output parameters

1. Do one or both of the following:
  - Specify a set of input parameters with values to be provided for the simulation.
  - Specify a set of output parameters to be obtained after the simulation is complete. If no output parameters are specified, all initialized values are returned. Request example for running the simulation with input and output parameters
2. Get the simulation results. Request example for getting simulation results

##### Car Braking Analysis sample

The *Car Braking Analysis* sample project demonstrates the server-side simulation that calculates stopping distance. You can use this project for the following scenarios:

- Seeing the relationship between the vehicle mass and braking distance.
- Calculating the stopping distance according to the car mass and speed.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Car Braking Analysis

To run the *Car Braking Analysis* sample to see the relationship between the vehicle mass and braking distance

1. Run the *Car Braking Analysis* project while changing the mass of the car, e.g., by 20 kg. Request example for running the simulation with a changed mass
2. Draw a chart of the relationship between the vehicle mass and braking distance. Request example for drawing the chart

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The chart showing the relationship between the vehicle mass and stopping distance.

To run the *Car Braking Analysis* sample to calculate the stopping distance

1. Run the *Car Braking Analysis* sample to calculate the stopping distance according to the specified car mass and speed. Request example for running the simulation to calculate the stopping distance
2. Draw a chart to show the car stopping distance with the threshold of the required maximum stopping distance. Request example for drawing the chart

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The chart showing the car stopping distance with the threshold of the required maximum stopping distance.

##### Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis sample

The Hinge Monte Carlo sample project demonstrates a server-side simulation that runs the Monte Carlo analysis. In addition, you can draw a histogram chart based on the CSV data after the model execution.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

Add the *Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis* sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.

To run the*Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis* and draw a histogram based on CSV data

1. Run the*Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis*and get the CSV data. Request example for running the simulation
2. Draw a histogrambased on the CSV data. Request example for drawing a histogram based on the CSV data

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The*Hinge Clearance Histogram*drawn based on the CSV data.

##### Cruise Control Widgets sample

The Cruise Control Widgets sample project demonstrates server-side simulation with a specified HTML UI mockup that includes widgets. With this project, you can open the HTML UI window during model execution.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

- Add the Cruise Control Widgets sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.
- To open the HTML UI during server-side simulation, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auto-generating HTML files' space=''] and commit the changes to the server .

To run the*Cruise Control Widgets*project and get the status of the specified simulation

1. Run the *Cruise Control Widgets*project. Request example for running the simulation
2. To get the status of the specified simulation. Request example for getting the status of the specified simulation/simulation/api/ui/160e822a-5c22-4758-9f1d-d72a83351559/SimulationWithWidgets.html'],
 'project': 'CruiseControl_Widgets',
 'config': 'Cruise Control with Widgets',
 'elapsedTime': 1657}]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The HTML UI window opened during the Cruise Control Widgets project execution.

**Sample projects**

- [ATTACHMENT filename='BouncingBall.ipynb']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='CoffeeMachine.ipynb']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='SpacecraftMassRollup.ipynb']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='CarBrakingAnalysis.ipynb']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.ipynb']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='CruiseControlWidgets.ipynb']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Once you set up Jupyter Notebook, you can open and test sample projects created for server-side simulation using Jupyter. This chapter briefly describes the sample projects and explains how to execute them.</p><p><br /></p><p>To access the sample projects</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Setting up Jupyter Notebook"><ri:page ri:content-title="Simulation using Jupyter Notebook" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Set up Jupyter Notebook]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> if you have not already done so.</li><li>Extract the &lt;<em style="text-align: left;">install_root</em><em style="text-align: left;">&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation\pyST.zip</em> file. The sample projects are located in the <em>samples</em> directory.</li></ol><h3>Bouncing Ball sample</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The <em>Bouncing Ball</em> sample project demonstrates server-side simulation with FMU. In addition, you can draw a chart based on the CSV data after the model execution. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f9e22db-5375-4835-9560-0e4f7ed34add"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Add the Bouncing Ball sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br />To run the <em>Bouncing Ball</em> sample and draw a chart based on the CSV data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Run the <em>Bouncing Ball</em> project and get the CSV data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="450a45e1-0ee3-4711-9879-211e08462766"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[result = client.simulate('BouncingBall", config='Run BouncingBall')
csv = result['csvExports']['Bouncing Ball']]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Draw a chart based on the CSV data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f9f724e-cdb9-45d8-b14d-1f22da433201"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for drawing a chart based on the CSV data</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot import figure
from io import StringIO

figure(figsize=(16, 8), dpi=80
x = np.loadtxt(StringIO(csv), dtype='double', delimiter=',', skiprows = 1, usecols = (0)) / 1000
y = np.loadtxt(StringIO(csv), dtype='double', delimiter=',', skiprows = 1, usecols = (1))
plt.xlabel('time (ms)')
plt.ylabel('height')
plt.title('Bouncing Ball', fontweight="bold", fontsize=20)
plt.plot(x, y,)
plt.grid()
plt.show()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="bouncing_ball_chart.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;">The <em>Bouncing Ball</em> chart drawn based on the CSV data.</h6><h3><br />Coffee Machine sample</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The <em>Coffee Machine</em> sample project demonstrates server-side simulation with a specified HTML UI mockup. With this project, you can open the HTML UI window during model execution.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e57371f4-34bc-43bc-b4a8-b4611cb39bd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Add the <em>Coffee Machine</em> sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.</li><li>To open the HTML UI during server-side simulation, <ac:link ac:anchor="Generating and attaching HTML files to Teamwork Cloud projects"><ri:page ri:content-title="Auto-generating HTML files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[attach the HTML UI to the server project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/CM/?contextKey=committing-changes-to-teamwork-cloud&amp;version=latest">commit the changes to the server</a>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To run the <em>Coffee Machine</em> sample and open the HTML UI window</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Run the <em>Coffee Machine</em> project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7920f543-c4e6-46c8-b203-dcca01e368b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[client.run('CoffeeMachine', config='Coffee Machine Web')

{'state': 'INITIALIZING',
 'simulationId': '20a84d37-0aba-4e12-9f14-ca381a5952f4',
 'project': 'CoffeeMachine',
 'elapsedTime': 106}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Check the simulation status.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1d6ea40-afbe-4c41-ab51-1e6e3f6ba55b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for getting the simulation status</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[client.get_status('20a84d37-0aba-4e12-9f14-ca381a5952f4')

{'state': 'RUNNING',
 'simulationId': '20a84d37-0aba-4e12-9f14-ca381a5952f4',
 'simulationTime': '0 ms',
 'ui': ['<server_address>/simulation/api/ui/20a84d37-0aba-4e12-9f14-ca381a5952f4/CoffeeMachine.html'],
 'project': 'CoffeeMachine',
 'config': 'Coffee Machine Web',
 'elapsedTime': 72742}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Click the UI URL in the response to the status request to open the HTML UI window.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="coffeemachine_UI.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The HTML UI window opened during the <em>Coffee Machine</em> project execution.</h6><h3><br />Spacecraft Mass Rollup sample</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The <em>Spacecraft Mass Rollup</em> sample demonstrates server-side simulation with specified input and output parameters.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7be87388-c7c1-4983-b705-e7dd874544a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Add the Spacecraft Mass Rollup sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To run the <em>Spacecraft Mass Rollup</em> sample with input and output parameters</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one or both of the following:<ul><li>Specify a set of input parameters with values to be provided for the simulation.</li><li><p>Specify a set of output parameters to be obtained after the simulation is complete. If no output parameters are specified, all initialized values are returned.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a94da246-c32d-4507-afc1-531f0b4ec0c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation with input and output parameters</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[parameters = {
 "inputs":
   {
        "propulsion.thruster.me":32
        "telecom.amplifier.me":15
   },
 "outputs":
    [
        "me",
        "propulsion.me",
        "propulsion.tank.me",
        "propulsion.thruster.me",
        "telecom.me",
        "telecom.antenna.me",
        "telecom.amplifier.me"
    ]
}

client.run('SpacecraftMassRollup', config='spacecraft mass analysis', data=json.dumps(parameters))

{'state': 'INITIALIZING',
 'simulationId': 'fc5fa7eb-761a-4d31-9201-2f51f1754675',
 'project': 'SpacecraftMassRollup',
 'elapsedTime': 187}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li><p>Get the simulation results.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a6b83af-2f64-47ca-9ab0-864be104dae4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for getting simulation results</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[client.get_result('fc5fa7eb-761a-4d31-9201-2f51f1754675')

{'outputs': {'me': 104.0,
  'telecom.me': 34.0,
  'telecom.amplifier.me': 15.0,
  'telecom.antenna.me': 19.0,
  'propulsion.me': 70.0,
  'propulsion.tank.me': 38.0
  'propulsion.thruster.me': 32.0},
 'verification': [{'property': 'propulsion.thruster.me',
   'status': 'fail',
   'value': 32.0,
   'requirements': [{'id': '1',
     'text': 'Estimated mass shall be less than allocated mass',
     'status': 'fail',
     'timestamp': 0}],
   'constraints': [{'constraint': 'me < ma',
     'status': 'fail',
     'timestamp': 0}]},
  {'property': 'telecom.amplifier.me',
   'status': 'fail',
   'value': 15.0,
   'requirements': [{'id': '1',
     'text': 'Estimated mass shall be less than allocated mass',
     'status': 'fail',
     'timestamp': 0}],
   'constraints': [{'constraint': 'me < ma',
     'status': 'fail',
     'timestamp': 0}]}]}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><br />Car Braking Analysis sample</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The <em>Car Braking Analysis</em> sample project demonstrates the server-side simulation that calculates stopping distance. You can use this project for the following scenarios:</p><ul><li>Seeing the relationship between the vehicle mass and braking distance.</li><li>Calculating the stopping distance according to the car mass and speed.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76615562-f0b7-44b0-ad4a-e247f1be010d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Add the <em>Car Braking Analysis</em> sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To run the <em>Car Braking Analysis</em> sample to see the relationship between the vehicle mass and braking distance</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Run the <em>Car Braking Analysis</em> project while changing the mass of the car, e.g., by 20 kg.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47e7284f-7619-400e-8a1a-53a74d3362de"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation with a changed mass</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mass = [0] * 10
distance = [0] * 10
for x in range(0, 5):
    mass[x] = 800 + 20 * x
    parameters = {
      "inputs":
      {
        "grossMass": mass[x]
      }
    }
    results = client.simulate('CarBrakingAnalysis_final', config='Vehicle Analysis no Matlab', commit_results=False, data=json.dumps(parameters))
    distance[x] = results['outputs']['stoppingDistance']
    print(mass[x], distance[x] )]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Draw a chart of the relationship between the vehicle mass and braking distance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5688e939-e2c9-4553-9a42-f68b097a0744"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for drawing the chart</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import numpty as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.pyplot import figure
from io import StringIO

figure(figsize=(16, 8), dpi=80)
plt.xlabel('mass (kg)')
plt.ylabel('stopping distance (m)')
plt.title('The relationship between vehicle mass and braking distance', fontweight="bold", fontsize=20)
plt.plot(mass, distance)
plt.grid()
plt.show()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="vehicle_mass_vs_braking_distance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The chart showing the relationship between the vehicle mass and stopping distance.</h6><p><br /></p><p>To run the <em>Car Braking Analysis</em> sample to calculate the stopping distance</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Run the <em>Car Braking Analysis</em> sample to calculate the stopping distance according to the specified car mass and speed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6fde3de-37a4-404f-93fe-f2717838d8ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation to calculate the stopping distance</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[speed = input("Enter the car speed (km/h):")
totalMass = input("Enter the car mass (kg):")

parameters = {
  "inputs":
  {
    "grossMass": totalMass,
    "speed": speed
  }
}

results = client.simulate('CarBakingAnalysis', config='Vehicle Analysis no Matlab', data=json.dumps(parameters))
distance = results['outputs']["stoppingDistance"]
requiredDistance = results['outputs']["requiredStoppingDistance"]
print("Stopping distance:", distance)
print("Failed requirements:")
print(json.dumps(results['verification'], indent=2))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Draw a chart to show the car stopping distance with the threshold of the required maximum stopping distance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f47443bd-7452-479b-b73b-9e4b91885b32"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for drawing the chart</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib as mpl
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

data = pd.DataFrame(
    {"distance": [distance]})
ax = data.plot(kind='bar'
              figsize=(10,7), color=['dodgerblue'], fontsize=13);
plt.axhline(y=requiredDistance, linewidth=3, linestyle='--', color='r')
ax.set_ylim([0, distance+50])
plt.title("car Braking Analysis Chart", fontsize=18, weight="bold")
plt.ylabel("Metre (m)", fontsize=15)
plt.text(0, distance+2, round(distance, 4), ha = 'center', fontsize=14)
ax.get_legend().remove()
plt.xticks(np.arange(1), ["Stopping distance"], rotation="horizontal", fontsize=15);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stopping_distance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The chart showing the car stopping distance with the threshold of the required maximum stopping distance.</h6><h3>Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis sample</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Hinge Monte Carlo sample project demonstrates a server-side simulation that runs the Monte Carlo analysis. In addition, you can draw a histogram chart based on the CSV data after the model execution.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="538933e4-c5a5-44a3-bfb3-8fd4bd9108bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisite</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Add the <em>Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis</em> sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To run the </span><em>Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis</em> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and draw a histogram based on CSV data</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p style="text-align: left;">Run the<span> <em>Hinge Monte Carlo Analysis</em> </span>and get the CSV data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1528fd86-b448-4105-a45d-214bd41ee5ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[result = client.simulate('HingeMonteCarloAnalysis_Jupyter', config='Monte Carlo Analysis')
csv = result['csvExports']['clearance']]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p style="text-align: left;">Draw a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">histogram </span>based on the CSV data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f79f78d3-b71f-406d-bcf9-821c40b3c5fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for drawing a histogram based on the CSV data</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.pyplot import figure
from io import StringIO

x = np.loadtxt(StringIO(csv), dtype='double', delimiter=',', skiprows = 1, usecols = (0))
numberOfBins = 30

figure(figsize=(16, 8), dpi=80)
plt.hist(x, bins = numberOfBins)
plt.xlabel('hinge.clearance')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.title('Hinge Clearance Histogram', fontweight="bold", fontsize=20)
plt.xticks(np.arange(min(x), max(x), 0.5))
plt.grid()
plt.show()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Histogram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;">The<span> </span><em>Hinge Clearance Histogram </em>drawn based on the CSV data.</h6><h3>Cruise Control Widgets sample</h3><p>The Cruise Control Widgets sample project demonstrates server-side simulation with a specified HTML UI mockup that includes widgets. With this project, you can open the HTML UI window during model execution.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d77c2833-7437-4b38-8402-4cfd760ba261"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisite</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Add the <em>Cruise Control Widgets</em> sample project to the Teamwork Cloud server.</li><li>To open the HTML UI during server-side simulation, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Auto-generating HTML files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[attach the HTML UI to the server project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/CM/?contextKey=committing-changes-to-teamwork-cloud&amp;version=latest">commit the changes to the server</a>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To run the </span><em>Cruise Control Widgets </em>project <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and get the status of the specified simulation</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Run the <em>Cruise Control Widgets </em>project.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1087c00-6f1a-4869-a881-08b239df94e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for running the simulation</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[result = client.run('CruiseControl_Widgets', config='Cruise Control with Widgets')
simID = result['simulationId']]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To get the status of the specified simulation.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="779e4536-7465-42a9-b731-8a53680197eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Request example for getting the status of the specified simulation</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[client.get_status(simID)

{'state': 'RUNNING',
 'simulationId': '160e822a-5c22-4758-9f1d-d72a83351559',
'simulationTime': '0 ms',
 'ui': ['<server_address>/simulation/api/ui/160e822a-5c22-4758-9f1d-d72a83351559/SimulationWithWidgets.html'],
 'project': 'CruiseControl_Widgets',
 'config': 'Cruise Control with Widgets',
 'elapsedTime': 1657}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cruise_Control_Sample.png" /></ac:image>The HTML UI window opened during the Cruise Control Widgets project execution.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample projects</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="BouncingBall.ipynb" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CoffeeMachine.ipynb" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="SpacecraftMassRollup.ipynb" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CarBrakingAnalysis.ipynb" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.ipynb" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CruiseControlWidgets.ipynb" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249569769 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00702: Saving plot results to the model

- page_id: `249569769`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249569769/Saving+plot+results+to+the+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:08:41.177+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > Time Series Chart
- labels: ['plot-results', 'saving-plot-results']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1417988244

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1417988246

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1417988245

The parameter **Result Location** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SimulationConfig stereotype' space='']<<SimulationConfig>> allows you to record and save plot results on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Series Chart' space=''] into the model when simulation is completed. You can save the plot results in the**Result Location**parameterin the following formats: PNG file, CSV, or HTML file by using the **Record Plot Data As** option in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of the Time series chart. The feature works with the Time series chart both stand-alone and in the UI diagram. You can then include the plot results into a generated document for reporting purposes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Saving plot results using resultLocation in the <<SimulationConfig>>.

To save plot results using **Result Location**

1. Open the Specification window of a <<SimulationConfig>> and set **Result Location** to an InstanceSpecification.
2. Open the Specification window of a TimeSeriesChart and set **Record Plot Data As** to the PNG, CSV, or HTML format.
3. **Run** the <<SimulationConfig>> until it terminates.****The expected plot result will be saved into the InstanceSpecification. You can review the result through specification of the InstanceSpecification.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If Result Location is set to a package, the plot results will be saved into a new InstanceSpecification at each runtime. However, if Result Location is set to an InstanceSpecification, the results will be saved into the selected InstanceSpecification.
- After the simulation stopped, the plot results (both image and CSV values) will be saved in the Image and Comment properties of the specified InstanceSpecification if the Record Plot Data As option in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Series Chart' space=''] is not specified.

The following figure shows the plot image saved in the Specification of Instance Specification of the bouncingBall model

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The plot image saved in the Image property of the specified InstanceSpecification of the bouncingBall model.

The image is too large to show in the **Image** property in the Specification window; therefore, you can verify it by dragging the InstanceSpecification into the diagram.

To verify a plot image saved in the Image property of the selected InstancedSpecification

1. Drag the InstanceSpecification to the diagram.
2. click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top left of the InstanceSpecification. The **Compartments** shortcut menu will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Compartments menu icon on the top left of the InstanceSpecification. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Select Stereotypes > Shape Image .

The following figure shows the CSV values saved in the Specification of Instance Specification of the bouncingBall model

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The plot CSV values are saved in the Body of the Comment property of the specified InstanceSpecification of bouncingBall model.

You can also specify the **Record Plot Data As** option in the Time series chart to your preferred plot result format (PNG, CSV, or HTML). If PNG is selected, you can verify the saved plot image by dragging the InstanceSpecification to the diagram and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top left of the InstanceSpecification to open the **Compartments** shortcut menu and select **Stereotypes** > **Shape Image**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Verifying the saved plot image of the InstanceSpecification via **Compartments** > **Stereotypes** > **Shape Image**.

If the **Record Plot Data As** option is HTML, the HTML source will be generated into the **Documentation/Hyperlinks** property group. You can customize the title, logo, font, and other layouts of the generated HTML source and use them in other reports.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML generated into the Documentation/Hyperlinks field of the InstanceSpecification.

The generated HTML can be saved and opened with a browser as shown in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The HTML files of the saved plot results (The Record Plot Data As option is HTML).

1417988243

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SimulationConfig stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Series Chart' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94e4c5fb-46f2-43b2-870e-201c78bca7eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1417988244</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c21189f5-496a-4e69-96ac-dc6fa38784e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1417988246</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaa6faeb-7e46-4f42-b997-e19a192842e2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1417988245</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="Standard" style="text-align: justify;">The parameter <strong>Result Location</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SimulationConfig stereotype" /><ac:link-body>&lt;&lt;<span class="inline-comment-marker">SimulationConfig&gt;&gt;</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> allows you to record and save plot results on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Time Series Chart" /></ac:link> into the model when simulation is completed. You can save the plot results <span>in the </span><strong style="text-align: justify;">Result Location</strong><span> parameter </span>in the following formats: PNG file, CSV, or HTML file by using the <strong>Record Plot Data As</strong> option in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the Time series chart. The feature works with the Time series chart both stand-alone and in the UI diagram. You can then include the plot results into a generated document for reporting purposes.</p><p class="Standard" style="text-align: justify;"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The saving plot results options." ac:alt="The saving plot results options."><ri:attachment ri:filename="BB_config.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Sa<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="614ab9d0-4edc-4129-8ba7-ea64764657cd">ving plo</ac:inline-comment-marker>t results using resultLocation in the &lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt;.</h6><p><br /></p><p>To save plot results using <strong>Result Location</strong></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the Specification window of a &lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt; and set <strong>Result Location</strong> to an InstanceSpecification.</p></li><li><p>Open the Specification window of a TimeSeriesChart and set <strong>Record Plot Data As</strong> to the PNG, CSV, or HTML format.</p></li><li><p><strong>Run</strong> the &lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt; until it terminates.<strong> </strong>The expected plot result will be saved into the InstanceSpecification. You can review the result through specification of the InstanceSpecification.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9391a67c-ac81-4938-9c83-58b97870459b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0660226-f394-4c13-8f58-0fa2102fec56">Result Locatio</ac:inline-comment-marker>n</strong> is set to a package, the plot results will be saved into a new InstanceSpecification at each runtime. However, if <strong>Result Location</strong> is set to an InstanceSpecification, the results will be saved into the selected InstanceSpecification.</li><li>After the simulation stopped, the plot results (both image and CSV values) will be saved in the <strong>Image</strong> and <strong>Comment</strong> properties of the specified InstanceSpecification if the <strong>Record Plot Data As</strong> option in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Time Series Chart" /></ac:link> is not specified.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="Standard">The following figure shows the plot image saved in the Specification of Instance Specification of the bouncingBall model</p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="savedimage_resultLocation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">The plot <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d5e962f7-36a9-44bb-9f49-53e0d09344e6">image</ac:inline-comment-marker> saved in the Image property of the specified InstanceSpecification of the bouncingBall model.</h6><p class="Standard"><span>The image is too large to show in the <strong>Image</strong> property in the Specification window; therefore, you can verify it by dragging the InstanceSpecification into the diagram.</span></p><p class="Standard"><span><br /></span></p><p class="Standard"><span>To verify a plot image saved in the Image property of the selected InstancedSpecification</span></p><hr /><ol><li class="Standard"><span>Drag the InstanceSpecification to the diagram.</span></li><li class="Standard"><p><span>click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments_menuicon.png" /></ac:image> on the top left of the InstanceSpecification. The <strong>Compartments</strong> shortcut menu will open. </span></p><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments_menu.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6>The Compartments menu icon on the top left of the InstanceSpecification.</h6><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments_menubox.png" /></ac:image></h6></li><li class="Standard">Select <strong>Stereotypes</strong> &gt; <strong>Shape Image</strong>.</li></ol><p class="Standard"><span><br /></span></p><p class="Standard"><span>The following figure shows the CSV values saved in the Specification of Instance Specification of the bouncingBall model<br /></span></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="the plot results saved as the Image propery of the specified InstanceSpecification." ac:alt="the plot results saved as the Image propery of the specified InstanceSpecification."><ri:attachment ri:filename="2016-11-22 12_42_36-Specification of Instance Specification bouncingBall at 2016.11.22 12.38.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">The plot CSV values are saved in the Body of the C<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9de21aff-b36f-4d40-a992-083b78d5e09b">omment</ac:inline-comment-marker> property of the specified InstanceSpecification of bouncingBall model.</h6><p class="Standard">You can also specify the <strong>Record Plot Data As</strong> option in the Time series chart to your preferred plot result format (PNG, CSV, or HTML). If PNG is selected, you can verify the saved plot image by dragging the InstanceSpecification to the diagram and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ba7aadd1-d110-44a3-be8e-976f548f8c23">click <ac:image><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9912162/compartments_menuicon.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1483601870291&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> on the top left of the InstanceSpecification to open the <strong>Compartments</strong> shortcut menu and select</ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong>Stereotypes</strong> &gt; <strong>Shape Image</strong>.</p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="HTMLplots.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Verifying the saved plot image of the InstanceSpecification via <strong>Compartments</strong> &gt; <strong>Stereotypes</strong> &gt; <strong>Shape Image</strong>.</h6><p class="Standard">If the <strong>Record Plot Data As</strong> option is HTML, the HTML source will be generated into the <strong>Documentation/Hyperlinks</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0a25ab86-da45-4a10-95a7-ee94659ee370">property group</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <span>You can customize the title, logo, font, and other layouts of the generated HTML source and use them in other reports.</span></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="HTML generated into Documentation/Hyperlinks field of the InstanceSpecification."><ri:attachment ri:filename="HML_documentationHyperlinks.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">HTML generated into the Documentation/Hyperlinks field of the I<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5f1b29c-4cfc-426f-8822-197f6fa71717">nstanceSpecification.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><p class="Standard"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5f1b29c-4cfc-426f-8822-197f6fa71717"><span>The generated HTML can be saved and opened with a browser as shown in the following figure</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="htmlplots_file.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">The HTML files of the saved plot results (The Record Plot Data As option is HTML).</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4cc6698-343e-4824-bc73-d8a4eb9f95e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1417988243</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SimulationConfig stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Time Series Chart" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571087 space=MMA version=6 -->
## PAGE 00703: Server-side simulation

- page_id: `249571087`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571087/Server-side+simulation
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-15T14:44:24.130+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you work with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can simulate them on the server without using a modeling tool. You can execute your models using REST API, or the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud user interface (if there are Cameo Collaborator documents published from those models). You can also run your models using the Resources application and Python client (pyST.py).

The following demo shows the server-side simulation in action:

800450

##### System requirements

The following Web Application Platform system requirements apply to an environmentthat runs a single server-side simulation. If you set up an environment where you can run concurrent server-side simulations, the system requirements applicable for a single simulation must be adjusted accordingly.

Web Application Platform system requirements for the environment with server-side simulation:

- Simple non-iterative simulations:
  - 1-core CPU
  - 1GB of RAM
  - Sample projects: <install_root>\samples\simulation\SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip <install_root>\samples\simulation\CoffeeMachine.mdzip
- Simple iterative simulations:
  - 2-core CPU
  - 1GB of RAM
  - Sample projects: <install_root>\samples\simulation\TradeStudyExamples.mdzip 
<install_root>\samples\simulation\ HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.mdzip
- Complex, time-consuming simulations:
  - 4-core CPU
  - 2GB of RAM
  - Sample project: [ATTACHMENT filename='ElectricityConsumptionAnalysis.mdzip']

##### Installation information

The server-side simulation service is one of many Web Application Platform services provided as a separate application. After successfully installing the server-side simulation service, the Simulation application is displayed on the welcome page of the Web Application Platform.Opening the application will redirect you to the Server-side Simulation Swagger page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Web Application Platform welcome screen

The server-side simulation service is installed automatically alongside Web Application Platform while using Teamwork Cloud or Magic Collaboration Studio installer. If you install Web Application Platform on a separate machine and use the Advanced Install option, select the server-side simulation service during installation. To learn more, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine' space='MCS'].

To install the Server-side simulation

1. Install Web Application Platform.
2. Open the < wap_*install_root*>\apache-tomcat\shared\conf \webappplatform.properties file and set the sim.license.checkout property to true .
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

##### Licensing information

The server-side simulation functionality only works with a floating Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit license managed by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DSLS installation and licensing' space='IL'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space='IL'].

You can setup the license either via the webappplatform.propertiesfile or the Web Application Platform. You can also configure the remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services. To learn more, click [here](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=advanced-services-configuration&version=latest&variant=default#Advancedservicesconfiguration-ConfiguringremoteFlexNetlicenseserverforWebApplicationPlatformservices).

1. In the Web Application Platform, select **Settings>** **Simulation Toolkit Web license**. To learn more about Setting application, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Settings application' space='MCS'] page.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click on the FlexNet tab, enter the license server name and click Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='FlexNet.png']
  - Click on the DSLS tab, select the Organization defined or Custom radio button, enter the license server name and click Apply . 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Concurrent simulations

Concurrent simulations

- If the license is set in the webappplatform.properties file, it cannot be configured via the Admin Console. To use the WebApp platform, remove the license entry from the properties file and restart the WebApp service.
- To be able to run concurrent simulations, deploy the server-side simulation service by using Kubernetes and Docker .
- When executing multiple simulations at the same time, every new concurrent simulation requires an additional Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit license.

##### Preparing projects for server-side simulation

To simulate a project on the server, it must meet the following criteria:

- A project has to be added to Teamwork Cloud.
- For simulation results to be saved, you need to specify the Result Location property of a Simulation Configuration.

To control the auto-starting and auto-terminating properties of server-side simulation, specify the**autoStart**and**autoTerminate**properties of SimulationConfig accordingly. To learn more, refer to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SimulationConfig stereotype' space=''].

##### Required permissions

To perform server-side simulation, you need to have the following Teamwork Cloud permissions:

- Read Resources - allows you to simulate resources.
- Edit Resources - allows you to commit the simulation results to a resource.
- **Manage Simulations** - allows you to manage all simulations, including the ability to get and review simulation results and terminate simulations executed by other users.

To manage all simulations, in addition to the Manage Simulations permission, you need to have the Read Resources permission for the related resources.

##### Server-side simulation limitations

There are several limitations related to server-side simulation:

- Math engine integrations (Matlab, Mathematica, etc.) are not available.
- FMU/FMU co-simulation with Windows binaries is not available.
- UseCase Execution is not available.
- DSL stereotypes are not applied to created Slot and InstanceSpecification elements.
- Finding elements by Name (e.g Signals, Operations with ALH, mapping with ExcelHelper) is not available.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you work with Teamwork Cloud projects, you can simulate them on the server without using a modeling tool. You can execute your models using REST API, or the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud user interface (if there are Cameo Collaborator documents published from those models). Y<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ou can also run your models using the Resources application and Python client (pyST.py).</span></p><p>The following demo shows the server-side simulation in action:<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7b1611d-60ca-4165-a483-0dccb7f0b75d"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B1-VA0u8TdU" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>System requirements</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The following Web Application Platform system requirements apply to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">an environment</span><span style="text-decoration: none;color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);"> that runs a single server-side simulation</span>. style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Web Application Platform welcome screen</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a84365c2-9f54-435e-9108-a8ee64bfc7b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The server-side simulation service is installed automatically alongside Web Application Platform while using Teamwork Cloud or To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To install the Server-side simulation</p><hr /><ol><li>Install Web Application Platform.</li><li>Open the &lt;<em>wap_<em style="text-align: left;">install_root</em>&gt;\apache-tomcat\shared\conf</em><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">\</span>webappplatform.properties</em> file and set the <strong>sim.license.checkout</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol><h3>Licensing information</h3><p>The server-side simulation functionality only works with a floating Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit license managed by the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="DSLS installation and licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DSLS]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FlexNet license server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can setup the license either via the webappplatform.properties</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> file or the <span>Web Application Platform</span>. <span>You can also configure the remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571083 space=MMA version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00704: Setting a cell value in an Excel spreadsheet

- page_id: `249571083`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571083/Setting+a+cell+value+in+an+Excel+spreadsheet

- version_date: `2017-10-06T07:16:24.727+02:00`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can assign an fUML runtime value to specific row and column in an Excel file by using the following API

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

